title: With an estimated 500,000 killed, the Russia-Ukraine war is Europe's deadliest since 1945
subhead: Despite the military secrecy surrounding casualties on both sides and the difficulty of counting civilian victims, efforts outside the battlefield and cross-checking of public data have made it possible to estimate the staggering human cost of four years of war.

title: Pakistan bombs Kabul in 'open war' on Afghanistan's Taliban government

Ukraine: 'The war could still last for years and paradoxically, time is not on the Kremlin's side'
subhead: Researcher Dimitri Minic tells Le Monde in an interview that it is 'too soon to write off Ukraine,' even though the balance of power currently favors Russia.

title: Netflix walks away from Warner Bros bid, clearing path for Paramount
subhead: The streaming giant's co-CEOs Ted Sarandos and Greg Peters said they were 'declining to match' Paramount Skydance's latest offer, which included a purchase price of $31 per share in cash, a one-dollar increase from its earlier bid, which valued the company at around $108 billion.

title: 'Jeffrey Epstein's trajectory shows that bluff and deceit remain commonplace'
subhead: Power holders cultivate a closed circle that shields them and reinforces a sense of power, seemingly legitimizing their abuses, sociologists François Denord, Paul Lagneau-Ymonet and Sylvain Thine write in an op-ed for Le Monde.

title: France's 'political violence has risen significantly, with assaults doubling over the past 10 years'
subhead: Sociologist Isabelle Sommier explains that mounting ideological fragmentation and political polarization, as well as increasingly brutal rhetoric, have fueled a surge in political violence, the majority of which is perpetrated by the far right.

How Paris is turning into 'Frollywood,' a refuge for American actors
subhead: Whether George Clooney, Matt Dillon or Kristen Stewart, France is attracting more and more actors from across the Atlantic. This trend speaks to both the Trump-era crisis and the decline of the Hollywood model.

title: Four years of war in Ukraine: Five photographers on assignment for Le Monde share their most striking images from 2025
subhead: Five photographers who covered the conflict in 2025 have each chosen an emblematic image of the conflict. Beyond the official figures and statements, these unique perspectives reveal the mark that this new year of war has left on them.

title: The Langs' troubling friendship with Jeffrey Epstein
subhead: Former French culture minister Jack Lang and his daughter Caroline Lang appear thousands of times in the most recently released documents. All evidence suggests that she had a much closer relationship with the sex offender than she now admits.
